fields.summary: Restore appToken and clientToken for app attempt after RM restart
[Umbrella] RM Restart Phase 1: State storage and non-work-preserving recovery

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

fields.description: These need to be saved and restored on a per app attempt basis. This is required only when work preserving restart is implemented for secure clusters. In non-preserving restart app attempts are killed and so this does not matter.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

comments.body: I get a feeling you should work on top of YARN-591, can you please do that? Tx.
This patch restores application token on restart. And add test on both memoryStore and FileSystemStore

This message is automatically generated.

I've only glanced over the patch, but do these tokens actually need to be handled specially?  Is it feasible to handle all tokens in an opaque credentials within the store?  I think that may reduce the copy-n-paste code throughout the stores for restoring these tokens.

bq.  Is it feasible to handle all tokens in an opaque credentials within the store?
Agreed. But because there are two types of tokens - application level and application-attempt level, we should have two credential fields.
Yes, application-level token is stored along with ApplicationSubmissionContext, no need additional handle for that

The RMStore stores applications and their attempts. And it is used to restore applications and their attempts from the data that they had earlier stored. This allows the recovery code to follow existing code paths to the fullest extent and prevent recovery logic from diverging from the "normal" code path. So I would like to avoid storing tokens separately from apps/attempts and then have to manage their relationship later on during recovery. As far as saving appToken and clientToken, I agree it would be nice to have a single object store all attempt tokens in one place. At AppSubmitContext does that for app tokens.
This patch restores appToken, clientToken will be addressed in another jira

Also, we could be better off storing credentials in rmappattemptimpl and only converting to bytebuffer inside rmstore. currently, because of this in amlauncher we end up converting bytebuffer back to credentials.

bq. RMAppAttemptImpl.appAttemptTokens should simply be Token<ApplicationTokenIdentifier>
Alternatively, it could Credentials so that no changes are needed for additional tokens.

bq. Alternatively, it could Credentials so that no changes are needed for additional tokens.
Inside the RM I'd like it to be explicit, so that we don't search in a credential cache each time we want a specific token.

The new patch:
1.cover restoring client token
2.move ApplicationAttemptStateDataPBImpl and ApplicationStateDataPBImpl to a new package resourcemanager.recovery.records.impl.pb
3.addressed previous comments

comments.body: I get a feeling you should work on top of YARN-591, can you please do that? Tx.
This patch restores application token on restart. And add test on both memoryStore and FileSystemStore

Console output: https://builds.apache.org/job/PreCommit-YARN-Build/836//console

This message is automatically generated.

I've only glanced over the patch, but do these tokens actually need to be handled specially?  Is it feasible to handle all tokens in an opaque credentials within the store?  I think that may reduce the copy-n-paste code throughout the stores for restoring these tokens.

bq.  Is it feasible to handle all tokens in an opaque credentials within the store?
Agreed. But because there are two types of tokens - application level and application-attempt level, we should have two credential fields.
Yes, application-level token is stored along with ApplicationSubmissionContext, no need additional handle for that

The RMStore stores applications and their attempts. And it is used to restore applications and their attempts from the data that they had earlier stored. This allows the recovery code to follow existing code paths to the fullest extent and prevent recovery logic from diverging from the "normal" code path. So I would like to avoid storing tokens separately from apps/attempts and then have to manage their relationship later on during recovery. As far as saving appToken and clientToken, I agree it would be nice to have a single object store all attempt tokens in one place. At AppSubmitContext does that for app tokens.
This patch restores appToken, clientToken will be addressed in another jira

Also, we could be better off storing credentials in rmappattemptimpl and only converting to bytebuffer inside rmstore. currently, because of this in amlauncher we end up converting bytebuffer back to credentials.

bq. RMAppAttemptImpl.appAttemptTokens should simply be Token<ApplicationTokenIdentifier>
Alternatively, it could Credentials so that no changes are needed for additional tokens.

bq. Alternatively, it could Credentials so that no changes are needed for additional tokens.
Inside the RM I'd like it to be explicit, so that we don't search in a credential cache each time we want a specific token.

The new patch:
1.cover restoring client token
2.move ApplicationAttemptStateDataPBImpl and ApplicationStateDataPBImpl to a new package resourcemanager.recovery.records.impl.pb
3.addressed previous comments
